title: Tangy, Fluffy French Comte Bouillabaisse
description: A midnight-craving french dish featuring comte cheese, served in a stone bowl and is whole30-friendly, similar to boeuf bourguignon. Perfect for a cold winter night.
instructions: Heat the stone bowl over medium heat for 5 minutes before adding the dish.
Add 2 tablespoons of olive oil to the bowl and sauté the diced onion and garlic until softened, about 3 minutes.
Add the sliced beef and cook until browned, about 5 minutes.
Add the diced tomatoes, beef broth, and herbs to the bowl and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 15 minutes.
Stir in the crumbled comte cheese until melted and smooth.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve the bouillabaisse hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
Divide the bouillabaisse into 4 equal portions and serve immediately.
Garnish with a dollop of sour cream and a sprinkle of paprika, if desired.
Serve with a side of crusty bread or crackers for dipping in the broth.
